text.heading: Buhari extols Onaiyekan’s virtues
text.p: A former Head of State, Major General Muhammadu Buhari (rtd), has congratulated the President of Christian Association of Nigeria (CAN) and Catholic Archbishop of Abuja, John Onaiyekan, on his 25th anniversary as a bishop.
Buhari, who noted Onaiyekan’s faultless dedication over the years, also acknowledged his "steadfastness and devotion" to the service of God and humanity.

In a statement, Buhari, who, praised the qualities of Onaiyekan as the chairman of the Catholic Bishops Conference of Nigeria, pointed out that his : "Liberation theology is anchored on his promotion and defense of the core values and basic tenets of democracy of one man, one vote that counts."

The statement reads in part: "Permit me on behalf of my family and men of goodwill nationwide, to extend profound congratulations to His Grace, Archbishop of Abuja and President of Christian Association of Nigeria (CAN), on this auspicious silver jubilee anniversary celebration as Bishop of our Lord.
"It is noteworthy to recall with admiration, the impeccable devotion of His Grace as servant of God Almighty over the years, for this I salute his steadfastness and devotion.

"May I also extol the liberation virtues of His Grace as the chairman of Catholic Bishops Conference of Nigeria and presently President of Christian Association of Nigeria (CAN), which in no small measure, has added another golden feather to his cap.
"His Grace’s liberation theology, is anchored on his promotion and defense of the core values and basic tenets of democracy; one man, one vote that counts.
"For his strident call for free, fair and transparent elections in Nigeria; I salute him, for his courage, I salute him and for his agitation for genuine democracy, I salute him immensely."
